response:
The award Alex Rodriguez won two of is the **Commissioner's Historic Achievement Award**. This award is presented by the Commissioner of Baseball to a group or person who has made a "major impact on the sport" of baseball. The most recent recipient is Derek Jeter (2014), who was honored in the final year of his career for being "one of the most accomplished shortstops of all-time." 

The award is presented by the Commissioner, and it is not an annual award. The trophy is a gold baseball sitting atop a cylindrical silver base, created by Tiffany & Co. The award has been presented fourteen times by Commissioner Bud Selig: eleven times to players, once to a team, and twice to a non-player. 

Alex Rodriguez was one of the recipients of this award, and he won it for his contributions to baseball.
